ending: Waybound brings the Cradle series to its close. The lion Monarch Reigan Shen is defeated and the ancient dreadgods that menaced the continent are finally brought to an end, though not without heavy cost. Lindon completes the climb he began as the powerless Unsouled of Sacred Valley and reaches the power of a Monarch, the strength he set out to gain when he first learned that his world was fated for destruction. With the immediate danger to Cradle resolved, Lindon and his closest companions - Yerin, Eithan, Mercy, and the others who traveled with him - leave the world behind and ascend beyond it into the greater universe, carrying their journey out of Cradle and into the wider cosmos that lies past it. The series ends with its central promise fulfilled: the boy once dismissed as worthless has become one of the most powerful beings his world produced, has saved what could be saved, and has passed on to whatever comes next. There is no further volume; the story of Lindon's rise on Cradle is complete.
in_short: In the final volume of Cradle, the ancient dreadgods rampage across the continent and the lion Monarch Reigan Shen makes his bid to seize supreme power by turning that catastrophe to his own ends. Lindon - once the despised Unsouled of Sacred Valley, now among the strongest sacred artists of his generation - races to complete his climb to the power of a Monarch, the strength he has pursued ever since he learned his world was doomed. With Yerin, Eithan, Mercy, Ziel, and his bonded companions at his side, he throws himself into a continent-spanning struggle against Reigan Shen and the dreadgods. The conflict is immense and costly, but Lindon reaches the pinnacle of the sacred arts and, together with his allies, defeats Reigan Shen and ends the dreadgod threat. With Cradle's crisis resolved, Lindon and his closest companions leave the world behind and ascend into the greater universe beyond it, completing the journey the series began.

recaps.text: Previously across the Cradle series: warned that his world was doomed unless he grew strong, Lindon rose from the despised Unsouled of Sacred Valley to the heights of the sacred arts, gathering companions - Yerin, Eithan Arelius, Akura Mercy, Ziel, and others - along with powerful allies and tools. After proving himself in the Uncrowned King tournament, where Yerin claimed the full legacy of the Sage of the Endless Sword and Lindon became the student of the Monarch Northstrider, he continued to climb toward the power of a Monarch himself. Throughout, he clashed again and again with the lion Monarch Reigan Shen and his dragons, while the ancient, near-unstoppable god-beasts known as the dreadgods grew active, threatening the continent and even Lindon's own homeland. By the time Waybound opens, the dreadgods and Reigan Shen's ambitions have driven events toward a final reckoning, and Lindon is racing to become strong enough to decide Cradle's fate.

Waybound opens with Cradle facing its gravest threat: the ancient dreadgods are active and devastating, and the lion Monarch Reigan Shen pursues a plan to turn that catastrophe to his own advantage and seize supreme power. Lindon, already among the strongest of his generation, drives himself toward the transformation into a Monarch, certain that nothing less will let him stand against the forces gathering over the continent. He and his companions - Yerin, Eithan, Mercy, Ziel, and his bonded spirits - marshal their strength and their alliances for a confrontation that will decide their world's fate.

Lindon and his allies commit to opposing Reigan Shen and containing the rampaging dreadgods, fighting to shield the continent's people from destruction. The struggle escalates into open conflict among the world's greatest powers, and Lindon presses relentlessly toward the pinnacle of the sacred arts. Each of his companions is tested as the separate battles begin to converge toward a single reckoning.

The conflict widens into a continent-spanning battle against Reigan Shen and the unleashed dreadgods. Lindon and his companions face powers far above their own, paying a heavy price to hold the line, as Lindon closes the final distance toward becoming a Monarch. Reigan Shen's full ambition stands revealed, and the fate of Cradle narrows to whether Lindon can reach the strength he has spent the whole series pursuing in time to matter.

The final confrontation arrives. Lindon completes his long climb and reaches the power of a Monarch, and he and his allies turn the full weight of their strength against Reigan Shen and the dreadgods threatening to destroy the continent. The battles are immense and costly, and the balance of the world hangs on their outcome.

By the end of Waybound, the final volume of the series, Reigan Shen is defeated and the threat of the dreadgods is brought to an end, at great cost. Lindon has become a Monarch, achieving the strength he set out to gain when he first learned that his world was in danger. With Cradle's immediate crisis resolved, Lindon and his closest companions leave the world behind and ascend beyond it into the greater universe, carrying their story out of Cradle and into the wider cosmos. The series closes with its central journey complete: the former Unsouled has saved what he could of his world and passed on to what lies beyond it.
